title: pityrogramma triangularis var. viscosa
intro: Pityrogramma triangularis var. viscosa, also known as the resinous tree fern, is a variety of epiphytic fern that is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its resinous sap. The resinous sap of Pityrogramma triangularis var. viscosa has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems, and is also believed to have antioxidant and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's resinous sap can be used to make salves, ointments, and infusions that are applied topically to the affected area.
